description: The landmark square in the center of Nanchang, featuring the August 1st Uprising Memorial Tower, located in the city's core area.
A thousand-year-old pagoda and an important cultural relic protection unit in Nanchang, surrounded by a folk culture street.
A historic park in Nanchang's urban area, a popular leisure spot for residents, ideal for family strolls.
An ancient Ming Dynasty structure standing by the river, rich in historical and cultural significance.
The former residence of late Ming Dynasty painter Zhu Da, a national first-class museum with an elegant garden-style ancient architectural complex.
A natural ecological park in the suburbs of Nanchang, a bird-watching paradise with fresh air.
Formerly known as Taiji Temple, the ancestral site of the Jingming sect of Taoism, located adjacent to the Memorial Hall of the Eight Eccentrics of Yangzhou.
The largest comprehensive museum in Jiangxi Province, showcasing the essence of Ganpo culture and historical artifacts.
An important revolutionary memorial in China, displaying the history of the August 1st Uprising and serving as a patriotic education base.
A key university in Jiangxi Province with a beautiful campus environment and a strong academic atmosphere.
